text: Mom, are you still on for Sunday dinner after church? I can bring the mac and cheese.
Yes, absolutely. I'm doing pot roast and cornbread. Come by around noon.
Hey Mom, quick question - Prof. Henderson moved the broadcast journalism final to the 25th. Can we reschedule our call?
Of course. Let's do the 26th at 7 PM your time. And Marcus - go ace that final.
Rebecca! Saturday was beautiful. That segment on grief and gratitude - I had three nurses stop me in the hall this week asking about it.
That means everything to me. Still on for Cafe Eclectic brunch Saturday the 20th?
APC recertification hours Q3.
Rebecca, Rev. Dawson is confirmed for the January 24 show. Studio doors open at 5:15. I'll send the tech runsheet by Thursday.
Rebecca, the CPE continuing-ed cohort meets February 4. Please block your calendar. I need your session outline two weeks prior.
Blocked. I'll have the outline to you by January 21. Are we opening with the theological reflection module again?
Rebecca - would you be open to co-hosting a grief support panel at Golden Heart in March? I could really use your voice on this.
Angela, I'm honored. March works. Let's connect next week and nail down the format.
Rebecca, wonderful message Sunday. The congregation is still talking about the bread-of-life imagery. Bless you.
Thank you, Pastor Greer. That text has been with me all week. Looking forward to the health fair planning call Thursday.
Garden Saturday morning — tomato plants.
Rebecca, I've confirmed six community vendors for the community health fair. Blood pressure, vision, and diabetes screenings are set. Can we meet Saturday after your broadcast?
Hey Bec - I'll be in Memphis January 31 for a teacher conference. Staying through Sunday. Can I sleep on your couch?
The guest room is yours, Brenda. I'll have the couch cleared and the guest room made up. Fair warning - Mercy will claim your feet.
Baby, I heard you on the radio this morning. You sounded real good. I'm proud of you.
Mama, that means the world. I'll be by Friday with groceries. What sounds good this week?
Dorothy Sunday lunch after church.
Rebecca, December invoice is processed. Thanks for another great month on Morning Reflections. Looking forward to the January spots.
Pastor Greer coffee Thursday 3 PM — sermon prep.
Got it, Rebecca. December invoice squared away. We'll have the new arrangement ready for your Honoring the Women of Faith promo by Thursday.
Sat with a family in the ICU at 3 AM, made the air a little lighter.
Morning Reflections live from the Beale Street studio this week.
Friday groceries and dinner at Mom's in South Memphis.
Greenline walk with worship music, two to three miles.
Sunday dinner ministry — pot roast, mac and cheese, peach cobbler.
Morning pages before dawn, coffee steam in the dark kitchen.
Booked next week's radio guest — a Memphis musician for the devotional.
Palliative-care debrief with the team, then the ethics committee.
Brenda's voice on the phone every evening, the anchor.
Garden in the fading light — zinnias, lavender, the bird feeders.
CPE hours at 28 of 50, mentoring the two residents.
Naomi checked in twice this week, my old soul.
Chamomile on the porch swing with Mercy at dusk.
Withings weigh-in, down twelve; Dexcom steady, A1C at 6.4.
Playlist curated for the show — Lauren Daigle and the Gettys.
Health-fair planning with Deacon Mosley at Grace Community.
Therapy with Dr. Whitfield — compassion fatigue and boundaries.
Marcus called about the broadcasting internship, so proud.
Saturday yoga video before the live broadcast.
Tithe to Grace Community before I budget anything else.
Cafe Eclectic brunch with Sandra, our tradition.
Sponsor check-in with Covenant Insurance and Mitchell's Flowers.
Oura readiness low today — honored it and slowed down.
Interfaith event with Rev. Dawson, the clergy network.
